sanskrit:
सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव।
न हि ते भगवन् व्यक्तिं विदुर्देवा न दानवाः॥
transliteration:
sarvam etad ṛtaṁ manye yan māṁ vadasi keśava |
na hi te bhagavan vyaktiṁ vidur devā na dānavāḥ ||
literal_meaning: All this (that) You tell me, Keśava, I accept as true; for neither the gods nor the demons, O Blessed One, know Your manifestation.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna expresses total cognitive surrender—his rational mind has aligned with revealed truth, dissolving epistemic anxiety and creating a secure base for action.
psychological_state: Secure-attachment to a transpersonal source of knowledge; ego boundaries soften without dissolving, allowing integration of faith and reason.
trigger_scenario: A client finally believes the therapist’s reframing of their trauma narrative—not because it is logically airtight, but because repeated experiential validation has built a secure therapeutic alliance.
anchor_text: Trust the divine mystery beyond mind, then move forward free of doubt.
